---
title: Arrays on GPUs
description: An introductory course to [![](img/logo_cupy.png){width="4.5em"}](https://github.com/cupy/cupy)
title-block-banner: true
---

:::{.topdef}

[CuPy](https://github.com/cupy/cupy) is a high-level library that provides a drop-in replacement for [NumPy](https://github.com/numpy/numpy) and [SciPy](https://github.com/scipy/scipy). In this course, we will see how to convert code from NumPy to CuPy and how to write GPU kernels when existing routines aren't sufficient. While doing so, we will benchmark executions on CPUs and GPUs and monitor GPUs usage.

This course is introductory and doesn't require any knowledge besides basic Python. Experience with NumPy is useful, but not necessary.

:::

<br>
[[Start course ➤](cupy_setup.qmd)]{.topinline}

<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "LearningResource",
  "name": "Arrays on GPUs with CuPy",
  "description": "CuPy is a high-level library that provides a drop-in replacement for NumPy and SciPy. In this course, we learn how to convert code from NumPy to CuPy and how to write GPU kernels when existing routines aren't sufficient. While doing so, we benchmark executions on CPUs and GPUs and monitor GPUs usage.\n\nThis course is introductory and doesn't require any knowledge besides basic Python. Experience with NumPy is useful, but not necessary.",
  "url": "https://mint.westdri.ca/python/gpu_cupy",
  "learningResourceType": "course",
  "inLanguage": "en",
  "license": "https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/",
  "provider": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "SFU Research Computing Group"
  },
  "keywords": "Python, GPU, CuPy",
  "author": {
    "@type": "Person",
    "name": "Marie-Hélène Burle"
  }
}
</script>
